news_summary: Netherlands drew Japan 2-2 and showed defensive frailty; Sweden beat Tunisia 5-1 and are expected to keep Isak-Gyokeres together. RotoWire lists no Dutch players on the injury table in the lineup notes, but its related player news flags Frenkie de Jong as in question.
model_impact: Raises Sweden attacking threat and match volatility; still no A-tier because Sweden handicap carries underdog/blowout risk and total over does not clear the model edge gate.

news_summary: Germany opened with a 7-1 win over Curacao. Ivory Coast beat Ecuador 1-0, but Evan N'Dicka is not expected to be fit and Elye Wahi will miss the match.
model_impact: Downgrades Ivory Coast +1 from watch-only value; injuries weaken the protected-underdog case. Germany -1 still does not clear model price.

news_summary: Both teams lost openers; Ecuador lost 1-0 to Ivory Coast, Curacao lost 7-1 to Germany. No Ecuador or Curacao players appear on the injury table in the lineup notes.
model_impact: Keeps Ecuador favored but makes deep handicap risky. Under 3 remains the cleanest A-tier because it has push protection at exactly three goals.

news_summary: Tunisia lost 5-1 to Sweden and need a result; Japan drew Netherlands 2-2. Takefusa Kubo is expected to miss out with a knee issue, with Junya Ito likely replacing him.
model_impact: Japan still stronger, but Kubo absence lowers creative certainty. Tunisia desperation increases volatility; no side or total reaches A-tier.
